logmel.window: center-crop or zero-pad to fixed_frames, then per-clip standardize (x-mean)/(std+1e-5)

exp03_soundtype.decision: predict 'cat' only if prob[cat] >= cat_threshold (in the .pt checkpoint, ~0.80). Below threshold => 'no result / hard to tell'. Conservative on purpose.

mfcc.vector: concat[ mfcc.mean(20), mfcc.std(20), delta.mean(20), delta.std(20), spectral_centroid.mean, spectral_centroid.std, zcr.mean, zcr.std, duration_sec ] = 85 dims
mfcc.note: librosa defaults (n_fft 2048, hop 512) over the full clip at 16 kHz mono. Matches EXP-01/EXP-02 feature. Standardize is inside the ONNX graph.

cat_dictionary.front_end: mfcc-85 (identical to exp02_affect; compute the MFCC-85 vector once and feed both cat_affect.onnx and cat_dictionary.onnx)
cat_dictionary.decision: argmax over the 8 classes is the top sound type; surface the full sorted list. Only meaningful when the cat detector accepts the clip (isCat). Confidence stays qualitative (clean/most-likely/possibly/hard-to-tell), never a percentage.

note: STFT/mel intentionally not in-graph (ORT-Web WASM STFT support is unreliable). Client computes features with these exact params then runs the ONNX model. Same split YAMNet/PANNs mobile ports use.
